 {"id":"cass_memory_system-bbb","title":"[Utils] hashContent - Normalized content hashing for deduplication","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:48.046449-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:48.046449-05:00","comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-bbb","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function hashContent(content: string): string
```

## Implementation Details
- Normalize content:
  - Convert to lowercase
  - Collapse multiple whitespace to single space
  - Trim leading/trailing whitespace
- Create SHA-256 hash using crypto.createHash('sha256')
- Update hash with normalized content
- Return first 16 characters of hex digest

## Why This Matters
Critical for exact duplicate detection in curator. Normalization ensures "Test Content" and "test  content" produce same hash, catching duplicates that differ only in formatting. Used in deduplication pass before semantic similarity check.

## Edge Cases to Handle
- Empty string - returns hash of empty string
- Only whitespace - normalized to empty, returns hash of empty
- Very long content - SHA-256 handles any size
- Unicode characters - ensure proper encoding (UTF-8)
- Newlines and tabs - collapsed to single space by normalization","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-bcj","title":"[Generator] executeSearchPlan - Parallel cass searches","description":"Parallel execution of cass searches with deduplication.

SIGNATURE:
async function executeSearchPlan(queries: string[], config: Config): Promise<CassHit[]>

PURPOSE:
Execute all generated queries against cass in parallel, combine results, deduplicate, and return top hits sorted by relevance score.

FLOW:
1. Map queries to cass search promises with config.sessionLookbackDays
2. Execute all with Promise.all (parallel for speed)
3. Flatten results into single array
4. Deduplicate by source_path + line_number (same snippet from multiple queries)
5. Sort by relevance score descending
6. Return top N results (config.maxHistorySnippets or default 10)

CASS SEARCH PARAMETERS:
- limit: 5 per query
- days: config.sessionLookbackDays (default 90)
- agent: config.preferredAgent (optional filter)

DEDUPLICATION LOGIC:
Two hits are duplicates if:
  hit1.source_path === hit2.source_path && hit1.line_number === hit2.line_number
Keep the one with higher relevance score.

RESULT SHAPE:
Array<{
  session_path: string,
  agent: string,
  snippet: string,
  relevance: number,
  source_path: string,
  line_number: number
}>

ERROR HANDLING:
- If cass binary not found, throw error (caught by generateTaskContext)
- If individual query fails, log warning but continue with other queries
- Return empty array if all queries fail","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:54.025102-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:59:44.041487-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:59:33.686488-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-be2","title":"[Decay] analyzeScoreDistribution - Playbook health metrics","description":"Analyze the distribution of effective scores across all bullets in a playbook. Provides health metrics showing how many rules are excellent, good, neutral, or at-risk. Used by stats command to show playbook quality.

COMPLETE IMPLEMENTATION:
```typescript
/**
 * Analyze score distribution across all bullets.
 * Provides playbook health metrics and identifies problematic rules.
 */
function analyzeScoreDistribution(bullets: PlaybookBullet[]): {
  excellent: number;      // score > 10
  good: number;          // score 5-10
  neutral: number;       // score 0-5
  atRisk: number;        // score < 0
  distribution: number[]; // histogram buckets
  avgScore: number;
  medianScore: number;
  totalBullets: number;
} {
  const scores = bullets.map(b => getEffectiveScore(b));
  
  // Category counts
  const excellent = scores.filter(s => s > 10).length;
  const good = scores.filter(s => s > 5 && s <= 10).length;
  const neutral = scores.filter(s => s >= 0 && s <= 5).length;
  const atRisk = scores.filter(s => s < 0).length;

  // Histogram: buckets from -5 to 15 in steps of 2
  const buckets = Array(11).fill(0);
  scores.forEach(score => {
    const bucketIndex = Math.min(10, Math.max(0, Math.floor((score + 5) / 2)));
    buckets[bucketIndex]++;
  });

  // Statistics
  const avgScore = scores.reduce((sum, s) => sum + s, 0) / scores.length;
  const sorted = [...scores].sort((a, b) => a - b);
  const medianScore = scores.length % 2 === 0
    ? (sorted[scores.length / 2 - 1] + sorted[scores.length / 2]) / 2
    : sorted[Math.floor(scores.length / 2)];

  return {
    excellent,
    good,
    neutral,
    atRisk,
    distribution: buckets,
    avgScore: Math.round(avgScore * 100) / 100,
    medianScore: Math.round(medianScore * 100) / 100,
    totalBullets: bullets.length
  };
}
```

SCORE CATEGORIES:

Excellent (score > 10):
- Highly proven rules with strong positive signal
- Typically proven maturity with 8+ helpful, 0 harmful
- Core playbook rules that define best practices

Good (score 5-10):
- Solid established or proven rules
- Consistent positive feedback
- Reliable guidance with few issues

Neutral (score 0-5):
- New candidates or low-feedback rules
- Neither strongly positive nor negative
- May need more time to prove value

At Risk (score < 0):
- Rules with harmful marks outweighing helpful
- Candidates for demotion or deprecation
- Need review - may be toxic or obsolete

HISTOGRAM BUCKETS:
```
Bucket 0: score < -5
Bucket 1: -5 to -3
Bucket 2: -3 to -1
Bucket 3: -1 to 1
Bucket 4: 1 to 3
Bucket 5: 3 to 5
Bucket 6: 5 to 7
Bucket 7: 7 to 9
Bucket 8: 9 to 11
Bucket 9: 11 to 13
Bucket 10: score > 13
```

USAGE IN STATS COMMAND:
```typescript
const analysis = analyzeScoreDistribution(playbook.bullets);

console.log('📊 Playbook Health Report');
console.log(`Total rules: ${analysis.totalBullets}`);
console.log(`Excellent (>10): ${analysis.excellent} (${pct(analysis.excellent)}%)`);
console.log(`Good (5-10): ${analysis.good} (${pct(analysis.good)}%)`);
console.log(`Neutral (0-5): ${analysis.neutral} (${pct(analysis.neutral)}%)`);
console.log(`At Risk (<0): ${analysis.atRisk} (${pct(analysis.atRisk)}%)`);
console.log(`Average score: ${analysis.avgScore}`);
console.log(`Median score: ${analysis.medianScore}`);

// ASCII histogram
console.log('\nScore Distribution:');
analysis.distribution.forEach((count, i) => {
  const scoreRange = `${i*2-5} to ${i*2-3}`;
  const bar = '█'.repeat(Math.floor(count / 2));
  console.log(`${scoreRange.padEnd(10)} ${bar} (${count})`);
});
```

EXAMPLE OUTPUT:
```
📊 Playbook Health Report
Total rules: 42
Excellent (>10): 8 (19%)
Good (5-10): 15 (36%)
Neutral (0-5): 16 (38%)
At Risk (<0): 3 (7%)
Average score: 6.23
Median score: 5.50

Score Distribution:
-5 to -3   ██ (2)
-3 to -1   █ (1)
-1 to 1    ████ (4)
1 to 3     ████████ (8)
3 to 5     ██████ (6)
5 to 7     ██████████ (10)
7 to 9     ████ (4)
9 to 11    ████ (4)
11 to 13   ██ (2)
>13        █ (1)
```

HEALTH INDICATORS:

Healthy Playbook:
- 60%+ in good/excellent
- <10% at risk
- Median score > 5
- Distribution skews right (positive)

Warning Signs:
- 20%+ at risk
- Median score < 2
- Many rules in -3 to 1 range
- Few or no excellent rules

Critical Issues:
- 50%+ at risk
- Average score < 0
- Distribution skews left (negative)
- Indicates playbook decay or mismatch with codebase

DEPENDENCIES:
- getEffectiveScore()
- PlaybookBullet array

USED BY:
- stats command
- Playbook health dashboard (future)
- Automated alerts (future)
- Quality metrics API (future)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:59.865436-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:43.927482-05:00"}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-bjz","title":"[LLM] llmWithRetry - Exponential backoff retry wrapper","description":"Function signature:
async function llmWithRetry<T>(
  operation: () => Promise<T>,
  operationName: string
): Promise<T>

Configuration:
- maxRetries: 3
- baseDelayMs: 1000
- maxDelayMs: 30000
- retryableErrors: ['rate_limit_exceeded', 'server_error', 'timeout', 'overloaded']

Logic:
1. Try operation
2. If error thrown:
   a. Check if error type is retryable
   b. If yes and retries < maxRetries:
      - Calculate delay: min(baseDelayMs * 2^attempt, maxDelayMs)
      - Log retry attempt with delay
      - Wait
      - Retry operation
   c. If not retryable or max retries exceeded, throw error
3. Return result on success

Logging example:
'LLM operation extractDiary failed (rate_limit_exceeded). Retrying in 2000ms... (attempt 2/3)'

Use this wrapper around all LLM operations for resilience against transient failures.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:09.625399-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:11:16.252488-05:00"}

   c. If not retryable or max retries exceeded, throw error
3. Return result on success

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-bm7","title":"[Reflector] getIterationNote - Context for subsequent iterations","description":"function getIterationNote(iteration: number): string

- iteration 0: empty string
- iteration 1+: "This is iteration {N+1}. Focus on insights you may have missed in previous passes. Consider edge cases, anti-patterns, and subtle preferences."

PURPOSE:
Helps LLM find additional insights on subsequent passes

EXAMPLES:
- iteration 0: "" (first pass is broad)
- iteration 1: "This is iteration 2. Focus on insights you may have missed in previous passes. Consider edge cases, anti-patterns, and subtle preferences."
- iteration 2: "This is iteration 3. Focus on insights you may have missed in previous passes. Consider edge cases, anti-patterns, and subtle preferences."

WHY THIS WORKS:
- Iteration 0: LLM finds obvious patterns
- Iteration 1+: Prompt guides LLM to look deeper
- Specific guidance: "edge cases, anti-patterns, subtle preferences"

IMPLEMENTATION:
```typescript
if (iteration === 0) return ''
return `This is iteration ${iteration + 1}. Focus on insights you may have missed in previous passes. Consider edge cases, anti-patterns, and subtle preferences.`
```

Used in buildReflectorPrompt()","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:00.16202-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:04:57.012226-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:04:55.552238-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-bor","title":"[LLM] PROMPTS.validator - Scientific validation prompt","description":"Full prompt template for scientific validation:

PROPOSED RULE: '{proposedRule}'

HISTORICAL EVIDENCE FROM CASS (last 90 days):
{evidence}

INSTRUCTIONS:
Analyze whether the evidence supports or contradicts this rule.

Criteria:
- INVALID: Evidence shows this pattern FAILED multiple times
- INVALID: Rule is too generic/obvious to be useful
- VALID: Evidence supports it OR is neutral
- Consider refining the wording if evidence suggests improvements

Be specific in your reasoning, citing session paths when relevant.

---

GPT Pro innovation - validate against historical data before accepting.

Temperature: 0.2 (low for consistency)
Used in runValidator function.

This is the scientific method applied to playbook rules - test hypotheses against empirical data from cass sessions.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:53.957778-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:55:28.721934-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:54:50.567929-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-eov8","title":"[CLI:forget] addToToxicLog - Block future similar rules","description":"Implements the addToToxicLog function that appends toxic entries to the permanent block list.

FUNCTION SIGNATURE:
```typescript
interface ToxicEntry {
  bulletId: string;           // Original bullet ID
  content: string;            // Full bullet content
  contentHash: string;        // SHA-256 hash for exact matching
  embedding?: number[];       // Vector for semantic similarity
  reason: string;             // Why this is toxic
  timestamp: string;          // ISO 8601 timestamp
  invertedTo?: string;        // ID of anti-pattern if inverted
}

async function addToToxicLog(
  entry: ToxicEntry,
  path: string
): Promise<void>
```

PURPOSE:
Maintains a permanent log of toxic bullets that should never be re-learned. Future extraction checks against this log using both exact hash matching and semantic similarity.

STORAGE FORMAT (toxic_bullets.log):
```jsonl
{"bulletId":"abc-123","content":"Cache auth tokens in localStorage","contentHash":"a1b2c3...","reason":"Security vulnerability - tokens exposed to XSS","timestamp":"2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"}
{"bulletId":"def-456","content":"Skip input validation for performance","contentHash":"d4e5f6...","embedding":[0.12, -0.34, ...],"reason":"Caused SQL injection in prod","timestamp":"2025-01-16T14:22:00Z","invertedTo":"ghi-789"}
```

IMPLEMENTATION REQUIREMENTS:
1. Generate SHA-256 hash of normalized content
2. Generate embedding vector (if embeddings enabled)
3. Validate entry has required fields
4. Append to toxic_bullets.log (JSONL format)
5. Ensure atomic writes (temp file + rename)
6. Update in-memory toxic cache for fast lookups

BLOCKING LOGIC:
When new bullet extracted:
1. Hash exact match → reject immediately
2. Semantic similarity > 0.85 → flag for review
3. Check reason/context from log

PERFORMANCE:
- Lazy load toxic log on first access
- Cache in memory during session
- Index by hash for O(1) exact lookups
- Use FAISS/similar for fast semantic search

FILE SAFETY:
- Use file locking to prevent concurrent writes
- Append-only (never modify existing entries)
- Backup before first write in session

DEPENDENCIES:
- Hash generation (crypto.createHash)
- Embedding generation (if enabled)
- File I/O utilities with atomic writes
- In-memory LRU cache for performance

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Entries persisted durably to disk
- Future extractions blocked correctly
- Semantic similarity catches variations
- No data corruption under concurrent access","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:52:42.280397-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:52:42.280397-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-f03p","title":"[CLI:playbook] add - Manually add bullet","description":"Manually add a new bullet to the playbook.

Command signature:
```bash
cass-memory playbook add [options] "<content>"

Options:
  --category CAT     Category (required)
  --tags TAGS        Comma-separated tags
  --scope SCOPE      global or workspace (default: global)
  --workspace PATH   Workspace path if scoped
```

Implementation:
1. Validate inputs:
   - Content must be non-empty
   - Category must be valid (tools/workflow/best-practice/pitfall/etc)
   - If scope=workspace: workspace path must be provided
   
2. Create new bullet:
   ```typescript
   const bullet: Bullet = {
     id: generateBulletId(),  // b-{random}
     content: content,
     category: category,
     maturity: 'candidate',  // always start as candidate
     score: 0.5,             // neutral starting score
     scope: scope,
     workspacePath: workspacePath,
     tags: parseTags(tags),
     sourceSessions: [],     // no source for manual adds
     createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
     lastUpdated: new Date().toISOString(),
     deprecated: false
   }
   ```
   
3. Add to playbook:
   - Load existing playbook
   - Append new bullet
   - Save to disk
   
4. Display confirmation:
   ```
   Added bullet b-abc123 as candidate
   Content: {content}
   Category: {category}
   
   Use 'cass-memory playbook get b-abc123' to view details
   ```

Use cases:
- Seeding initial playbook before any sessions
- Adding external best practices
- Quick manual additions

Note: Manually added bullets start as candidates and need evidence to mature.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:52:12.696819-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:52:12.696819-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-f4lo","title":"[ErrorHandling] normalizePlatformPath - Cross-platform paths","description":"Normalize file paths for cross-platform compatibility.

Function signature:
function normalizePlatformPath(p: string): string

Implementation:
- Expand tilde (~) to user home directory
- Handle Windows backslashes vs Unix forward slashes
- Handle Windows drive letters (C:, D:, etc.)
- Normalize to platform-appropriate separator (path.sep)
- Handle UNC paths on Windows (\\server\share)
- Remove redundant separators
- Resolve . and .. components

Examples:
- Unix: '~/docs' -> '/home/user/docs'
- Windows: '~\docs' -> 'C:\Users\user\docs'
- Windows UNC: '\\server\share\file' -> '\\server\share\file'
- Cross-platform: '../file' -> absolute path

Ensures consistent path handling across platforms.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:29.132495-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:29.132495-05:00"}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-fh0","title":"[LLM] llmWithFallback - Multi-provider fallback","description":"Function signature:
async function llmWithFallback<T>(
  schema: z.ZodSchema<T>,
  prompt: string,
  config: Config
): Promise<T>

Implementation:
1. Try primary provider (from config.provider)
2. If fails:
   a. Check which other providers have API keys set
   b. Try them in order: anthropic → openai → google
   c. Use same prompt and schema
   d. Return first successful result
3. If all providers fail, throw combined error

Provider ordering rationale:
- Anthropic: Strong reasoning, structured output
- OpenAI: Fast, reliable
- Google: Fallback option

Resilience: Protects against provider-specific outages.

Use case: Production systems where uptime matters more than specific provider.

Note: Config should have a 'fallbackEnabled' flag - only use this if user opts in.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:10.574337-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:10.149482-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-fyry","title":"[ErrorHandling] handleRateLimit - Backoff on rate limits","description":"Implement intelligent rate limit handling with exponential backoff.

Function signature:
async function handleRateLimit(
  error: Error,
  attempt: number
): Promise<{ shouldRetry: boolean; delayMs: number }>

Implementation:
- Parse retry-after header from error if available
- Otherwise use exponential backoff: baseDelay * 2^attempt
- Cap at maxDelayMs (30 seconds)
- Log wait time to user ('Rate limited, retrying in 4 seconds...')
- Return false if max retries exceeded

Error detection:
- HTTP 429 status
- Error messages containing 'rate limit'
- Provider-specific rate limit errors

Ensures respectful API usage and automatic recovery from rate limits.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:50:49.746686-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:50:49.746686-05:00"}
